coverage_note: The bundled CHEK2 population database stores gene-level and marker-level interpretation notes, but does not embed a full ancestry-frequency matrix for these markers yet.
gene_population_patterns_intro: Broader population patterns curated from CHEK2 DNA damage checkpoint signaling, p53 and BRCA pathway crosstalk, cell-cycle arrest, and hereditary cancer modifier biology and condition-association literature.

sources.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000183765: CHEK2 coordinate source

gene_population_patterns.summary: CHEK2 findings should be read against DNA damage checkpoint signaling, p53 and BRCA pathway crosstalk, cell-cycle arrest, and hereditary cancer modifier biology; population frequency, ascertainment, and phenotype definition can change the apparent effect size.

CHEK2 c.1100delC model is curated as a CHEK2 marker for Hereditary cancer susceptibility context, DNA damage checkpoint biology; interpret ancestry and cohort patterns before assigning biological direction.

rs17879961 (CHEK2 I157T) is curated as a CHEK2 marker for Hereditary cancer susceptibility context, DNA damage checkpoint biology; interpret ancestry and cohort patterns before assigning biological direction.
